ops.reason: US-Iran-Israel 2-week ceasefire agreed April 7; acute war premium removed. Strait of Hormuz to reopen. Peace talks begin Islamabad April 10. Regime shifts from Geopolitical Shock to Transitional/Unclear — ceasefire is conditional, Lebanon excluded, peace not guaranteed.

ops.value: REGIME SHIFT: Geopolitical Shock → Transitional/Unclear. US-Iran-Israel 2-week ceasefire (April 7) removes acute war premium. Strait of Hormuz reopening drives oil -15% to $96. Equity futures ripping +2.5%. Gold holding near ATH on dollar weakness (DXY 98.84). VIX elevated at 27. Ceasefire is temporary (2 weeks), Lebanon excluded, and tariff/inflation headwinds persist. Risk: ceasefire breaks down before Islamabad talks (April 10) complete.
ops.reason: Ceasefire fundamentally changes macro regime from Geopolitical Shock. All 4 macro factors re-rated.

value.note: Equity rally suggests market pricing in relief from oil shock. But tariff headwinds remain (Section 232 metals 50%, pharma 100%). SCOTUS IEEPA ruling (Feb 20) limits scope but damage already done.

ops.reason: Ceasefire removes oil inflation shock, improving growth outlook at margin.

value.note: WTI -15% to $96 is a major disinflationary event. Fed rate cut expectations revived (Bloomberg: 'Treasuries Rise as Ceasefire Spurs Oil Drop, Fed Rate-Cut Hopes'). But metals/pharma tariffs add structural inflation. PCE/CPI path still uncertain.

ops.reason: Oil plunge removes biggest near-term inflation driver.

value.note: Fed funds: 3.50-3.75% (held at Jan 28 FOMC). No April FOMC scheduled. Next FOMC blackout April 18-30. Oil drop re-opens rate cut path. 10Y fell from 4.36% (April 7) to ~4.24% today.

ops.reason: Oil price collapse revives rate cut expectations.

value.note: Crypto F&G at 17 (Extreme Fear). VIX 27. S&P futures +2.5%. Conflicted: relief rally but deep uncertainty about ceasefire durability. Regime playbook for Transitional: 10% equity, 10% gold, 80% cash.

ops.reason: Relief rally present but fragile. VIX still elevated, crypto still in extreme fear.

spy.note: April 7 close. Ceasefire rally in progress.

ops.reason: Ceasefire day: WTI -15%, Gold +2.2%, DXY -1%, 10Y -12bps, BTC +4.1%, S&P futures +2.5%
Ceasefire is risk-on for equities, risk-off for energy geopolitical premium. Macro regime in flux.

ops.value: **REGIME SHIFT: Geopolitical Shock → Transitional.** The US-Iran-Israel 2-week ceasefire announced April 7 (effective April 8) is the dominant macro event. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz under coordination with Iranian armed forces. Peace talks begin in Islamabad April 10. This removes the acute energy disruption premium that has driven WTI to $112 and elevated VIX throughout W15. Key risks: (1) ceasefire breakdown before peace talks conclude — Israel says Lebanon excluded; (2) tariff structural inflation persists (Section 232 metals at 50%, pharma at 100%, SCOTUS ruled IEEPA tariff use illegal Feb 20 limiting future scope); (3) peace deal terms contentious — Iran demands sanction lift, asset release, US troop withdrawal, nuclear enrichment rights. **Regime playbook: Transitional/Unclear → 10% equity, 10% gold, 80% cash.** The oil shock has ended; now the question is whether the peace deal holds. Bias: cautious. Do not chase the energy rally lower or pile into equities until peace talks outcome is clearer (April 10+).
ops.reason: Ceasefire triggers full macro regime reassessment.
Ceasefire relief rally strong (+2.5% SPY futures) but earnings season starts and tariff/growth headwinds persist.

ops.value: **MASSIVE RELIEF RALLY.** S&P 500 futures +2.1-2.8% on ceasefire announcement. SPY closed $659.22 April 7; opening ~$673-677 April 8. The energy-driven inflation shock is abruptly reversed — WTI -$17 removes the primary bearish catalyst for consumer spending and corporate margins. **Sector implications:** Energy (XLE) is being sold hard; rotation into consumer discretionary, technology, and financials. Consumer staples (XLP) holding as defensive quality rather than war-hedge. Small caps (IWM) likely outperform as domestic growth outlook improves with lower energy prices. **Risks to the rally:** (1) Earnings season (Q1 2026) begins next week — tariff costs may be visible in margins; (2) VIX still elevated at 27, suggesting hedging remains active; (3) The ceasefire is 2 weeks — the rally is pricing in permanence it hasn't earned yet. **Bias: Bullish near-term (1-5 days), Cautious medium-term (1-4 weeks).** Confidence: Medium.
ops.reason: Ceasefire is unambiguously equity-positive in the short run; medium-term depends on peace deal outcome.

value.driver: Oil -15% on ceasefire (WTI $96 from $113). War geopolitical premium being priced out. XLE likely -8-12% session. T-001 thesis broken.

ops.reason: Ceasefire ends the geopolitical energy premium. Thesis break.

value.driver: XLP holding as defensive quality; less war-hedge premium now. Lower oil helps consumer margins. Maintain but reduce conviction.

ops.reason: XLP thesis partially intact (defensive quality) but war-hedge component gone.

value.driver: Relief rally favors high-beta growth. Tariff IEEPA ruling limits tech hardware tariff exposure. AI demand intact.

ops.reason: Risk-on regime shift benefits tech in near-term.
10Y fell 12bps on ceasefire (oil drop → lower inflation → rate cut bets). But structural tariff inflation limits upside for duration.

ops.value: **RALLY on ceasefire.** 10Y: 4.34% (April 6 close) → ~4.24% today (-10bps). 2Y: 3.84% → ~3.75% est. 2s10s spread ~49bps (positive slope maintained). The oil price collapse removes the biggest near-term inflation risk, reviving Fed rate cut expectations. Bloomberg: 'Treasuries Rise as Ceasefire Spurs Oil Drop, Fed Rate-Cut Hopes.' Fed funds 3.50-3.75% (unchanged since March FOMC). **Implications for portfolio:** BIL (40%) and SHY (15%) are working — yield income continues, and short-duration bonds are benefiting from the rate-cut narrative. Do NOT extend duration aggressively — tariff-induced structural inflation limits TLT upside. Bias: Bullish short duration, Neutral long duration. Confidence: Medium.
ops.reason: Oil-driven inflation relief is the dominant bond driver today.
WTI -15% on ceasefire/Hormuz reopening. Gold +2.2% — dollar weakness (DXY 98.84) offsets geopolitical safe-haven unwind.

ops.value: **OIL CRASHES, GOLD HOLDS.** WTI: $112.95 (April 7 close) → $96.32 intraday, range $91.11-$109.00 (session low of $91 tested). Brent: ~$92 (-16%). The Strait of Hormuz reopening removes the supply disruption risk premium that drove WTI to $112. **Gold:** Spot $4,803.83 (+2.2%), futures $4,835.90 (+3%). The gold move is COUNTERINTUITIVE — war premium partially unwinds, but USD weakness (DXY -1% to 98.84) and rate cut expectations are providing a bid. Gold is behaving as a dollar hedge today, not just a war hedge. **Portfolio implication:** DBO stop loss ($18/WTI ~$95) TRIGGERED. WTI tested $91 intraday. Exit DBO. IAU: gold is holding near ATH — thesis has partially evolved from pure war hedge to dollar/inflation hedge. Review whether to hold or reduce IAU. Bias: Bearish oil, Neutral/cautiously-bullish gold. Confidence: High (oil), Medium (gold).
ops.reason: Oil ceasefire crash is clear; gold thesis evolution requires PM assessment.
DXY fell to 98.84 (4-week low). Oil inflation fears eased, rate cut bets revived, risk-on sentiment.

ops.value: **USD WEAKENS SHARPLY.** DXY: 98.84 (four-week low, range 98.84-99.72). Dollar weakened as: (1) oil inflation fears eased, (2) rate cut expectations revived, (3) risk-on flow. EUR/USD likely bouncing; USD/JPY falling. USD/CAD: Positive for the portfolio (holdings denominated in USD, base currency CAD — dollar weakness vs CAD reduces NAV). **Key level:** DXY 97-98 is the next support zone. If ceasefire holds, dollar could slide further as the 'inflation shock' premium unwinds. Bias: Bearish USD short-term. Confidence: Medium.
ops.reason: DXY fell below 99 on ceasefire relief.
EM stocks rallied most since 2022 on ceasefire. Strait of Hormuz reopening benefits EM commodity importers. Dollar weakness supports EM assets.

ops.value: **EM RIPS.** Emerging market stocks rallied most since 2022 on ceasefire news. Dollar weakness (DXY 98.84) is a direct EM tailwind — reduces USD-denominated debt service costs and supports commodity-importing economies. Japan (EWJ): yen strengthening on USD weakness. Europe (EWG/VGK): benefiting from lower energy costs (European energy crisis thesis eases). China (FXI): watch for strategic implications of US-Iran deal on Chinese energy diplomacy. **Key watchlist names:** EEM, VWO, EFA — all likely outperforming SPY today. **Not a current holding — flag for opportunity screener.** Bias: Bullish. Confidence: Medium.
ops.reason: EM historically outperforms in risk-on, dollar-weak environments.
BTC +4.1% to $71,600, ETH +5.6% to ~$2,250. Risk-on catalyst. But F&G still 17 (Extreme Fear) — not a conviction buy.

ops.value: **RISK-ON BOUNCE, NOT A REGIME CHANGE.** BTC: ~$71,600 (+4.1%). ETH: ~$2,250 (+5.6%). Total market cap: $2.52T (+4.3%). The ceasefire triggered a risk-on bounce that lifted crypto along with equities. However, the Fear & Greed Index remains at 17 (Extreme Fear, up from 11 yesterday) — institutional positioning is still defensive. **Context from April 6-7:** Bitcoin was trending down ahead of the ceasefire deadline, trading near $69K. The bounce to $71.6K is a relief move, not a new bull leg. Key resistance: $73-75K (the previous range top). Support: $67-68K. **Not a portfolio holding.** Bias: Cautiously bullish near-term. Confidence: Low (ceasefire-dependent).
ops.reason: Crypto is a leveraged risk-on instrument. Ceasefire helps but F&G at 17 signals structural caution.
Crypto F&G: 17 (up from 11). Still Extreme Fear regime.

ops.value: **EXTREME FEAR, IMPROVING.** Crypto Fear & Greed: 17 (Extreme Fear), up from 11 (yesterday) and 8 (last week). The index is rising but far from neutral (50). Sentiment surveys and social data: ceasefire news should push a wave of retail relief, but professional/institutional positioning remains cautious (VIX 27 confirms). CTA positioning: systematic trend-followers may have been short energy/long defensive — the ceasefire creates a large positioning squeeze in energy shorts and defensive longs. Options: Energy vol likely spikes (gamma exposure on oil move). **Implication:** The fear gauge improving from extreme levels is historically bullish on a 1-4 week horizon, but requires sustained positive news flow (peace talks success).
ops.reason: F&G improved meaningfully but remains in extreme fear zone.

value.name: Iran War Geopolitical Premium — Energy & Gold
value.note: US-Iran-Israel 2-week ceasefire agreed April 7. Strait of Hormuz reopening. WTI -15% to $96 (from $113). The acute war premium that drove XLE and DBO positioning has been removed. This is a THESIS BREAK, not a temporary wobble. The peace talks may fail and the war could resume — but the rule is: thesis break = exit. Gold sub-thesis partially survives as dollar/inflation hedge (IAU +2.2% on DXY weakness). Energy sub-thesis (XLE, DBO) is broken.
value.action: EXIT DBO (stop triggered, WTI below $95 intraday). REDUCE XLE materially. Review IAU — partial hold possible if dollar-weakness thesis holds.

ops.reason: Ceasefire removes the geopolitical war premium that justified energy positioning.

value.action: Increase from 40% → 60%. Transitional regime = cash heavy. 5%+ yield. Absorb XLE and DBO exits.
Reduce from 20% → 15%. Gold ATH at $4,803. War premium partially unwinds; dollar-weakness thesis supports a partial hold. Take 5% profit into ceasefire strength.
Hold at 15%. Rate cut narrative supports short duration bonds.
Increase from 8% → 10%. Defensive quality in transitional regime. Lower oil supports consumer staples margins. Modest add.
EXIT full 12% position. T-001 energy thesis BROKEN. War premium gone. WTI at $96 and falling. Do not hold a position whose thesis has been invalidated.
EXIT full 5% position. Stop loss triggered (WTI tested $91, stop was WTI ~$95/$18 NAV). T-001 broken.

ops.reason: Regime shift to Transitional/Unclear. T-001 energy thesis broken. Cash increase to 60% per regime playbook (80% target, compromised by IAU/SHY/XLP partial holds with intact sub-theses).

notes: REGIME SHIFT DAY. US-Iran-Israel 2-week ceasefire (April 7, effective April 8) is the dominant event. WTI -15% to $96, S&P futures +2.5%, gold +2.2%, DXY -1%. T-001 (energy geopolitical premium) BROKEN. Portfolio triggers fired: (1) DBO stop loss (WTI $91 intraday vs $95 stop), (2) T-001 thesis break, (3) macro regime shift. Full PM review conducted. Recommendation: EXIT XLE + DBO, REDUCE IAU 20→15%, ADD XLP 8→10%, INCREASE BIL 40→60%. Regime playbook Transitional/Unclear target is 80% cash — current recommendation at 60% BIL + 15% SHY = 75% cash-like, close to playbook. Peace talks begin Islamabad April 10 — next reassessment trigger.
